I have nothing but utter hatred for this game. Every time I come back to play it on a different platform I keep thinking it wasn't as bad I remembered it but it somehow just elevates itself further and further. Does not help that this is a Capcom port in the era of them being absolutely horrendous at them AND with me having an UHD monitor which does not like this game at all.

Gameplay wise? Yikes. This is by far the worst experience you can have in the mainline of Resident Evil. There are so many instances of just losing control of your character and it taking far too long for you get it back. Leon's campaign is riddled with these little stumbling set pieces. Having the camera get ripped out of your control to look at something you don't need to know about because it's a linear ass game with a button you can press to see where to go. Only for you to realize that the enemies can move and will hit right as you get out of the cutscene. Usually meaning an instant down on higher difficulties. Speaking of getting down. What's the point of having a Last Stand mechanic if it takes you 8 to 10 seconds for it to finally let you use your gun while their enemies are around you. I also can not in enough words express how tired I am of transforming enemies AND enemies with guns. This game ramps that up to 11 in some of the later chapters.

The UI in this game. Holy hell. RE5 with it's plethora of weapons was fine because you just chose which weapons you wanted. You'd have 2 types on Chris and 2 types on Sheva. Then you'd have room for ammo, which stacked high, and didn't have certain ammo types dropping. This game? Let's give you ALL the weapons AND the ammo has dinky stack counts. Constantly having to sit there and futz around with the awful UI to drop things to pick up a box of ammo to just immediately reload the gun I actually want to use is just poor planning. There's no reason why the ammo stack count should be so low or the inventory being so limited.

The campaigns drag on and the overall story is just some dude who had Yellow Fever and was transforming women into an Asian woman who then got pissed and decided to destroy the world. Just nonsense. Spread along 4 campaigns where, for most of them, not much really happens. Not to mention Ada's campaign is a questionable tacked on addition where only her first mission has any true added relevancy to the plot and they couldn't even properly do a story where she would have a co-op partner.

If you're desperate for a co-op game and somehow haven't played this game yet BUT you've already played RE5 then sure get it at 5 dollars. Just temper your expectations and you might have an okay time while you question the game and the decisions they've made along with your buddy.

Oh, and if you have an Ultrawide then get Flawless Widescreen. It'll *sort of* fix the issues with the ugly UI.

I realize this review is 13 years too late, but I felt compelled to write something about this game. Let me begin by saying that I respect the vision and the work put into it. This game is not unplayable, but it is far from being good. I simply love the creative body horror creature designs and also the vamped up dodging and melee system, to name a few of the positives. I just wish all the good aspects of RE6 were found in a single long campaign. Had RE6 been solely about Jake, there would have been room to refine the story and gameplay further. 

Obviously a lot of thinking went into this project, and I get the impression there was true Herculean effort involved in making it work. Sadly all that energy is misguided, as RE6 is a product of it's time and not in a good way. The reason I came here to write about RE6 is that this essentially marked the end for "classic" campy RE titles which I have come to sorely miss. I may have hated this game when it was new, but that was because I had no idea how bad it would eventually get with endless remakes and reboots. After finally having beaten this godforsaken game, coming back to it after 10 or so years with the slightly easier KB+M controls, I have come here to pay my respects and grieve the over-the-top Michael Bay Transformers Movie death of the identity of Resident Evil.

This game copies nearly all the trends of 2010 gaming in a very cheap way, and this was also around the time of peak Call of Duty hype. RE6 decided to go all-in on the popularity of CoD and Battlefield, and the heat from the glowing embers of the cultural strangehold that those series had on the world at the time of this game can still be felt to be blazing hot on one's aching retinas if you turn your brain off. I specifically remember thinking back then: "This game is trying to be CoD so hard." Every bit is calculated down to the last detail to be a slam dunk, but there is simply not enough meat around the bone. An interactable movie is the closest description that I can offer, or as one negative review here stated shortly but brilliantly; "it's like gears of war if it was bad." This would have been better simply AS a movie, however stupid the plot, because the impression I get based on the uninspired levels is that the people in charge of gameplay simply didn't have the skill to make the vision work, however what IS there is already quite impressive.

I suspect the megalomaniacal plans for RE6 sounded good, but in the end it just came out half-baked in the middle and burnt on the sides. They just started adding features and didn't know where to stop. There is online co-op and perk systems like Call of Duty, horde mode a la Gears of War, multiple campaigns and characters because people loved it in RE4, replayability that will encourage players to hang around, and heaps upon thrice damned heaps of instadeath QTE's and control being constantly ripped away from the player after literally 5 steps for the sake of being "cinematic". Do not even think I am joking about those 5 steps either, because it happens so often I began counting the steps. The usual flow of the story campaigns are; cutscene ->give control to player ->make them walk 5 steps ->rip away camera control to show some important setpiece ->2 or so minutes of gameplay that is interrupted by cutscenes that doesn't have time to become fun before a Crash Bandicoot style instadeath chase sequence interrupts flow ->yet another door or cutscene but multiply that by 10 ->level ends. If you skipped every cutscene and QTE you would be left with 30 minutes of gameplay per campaign which are all around 4-5 hours long on Normal.

RE6's effort is misguided, but the campy soul is still there, even if it tries to constantly one-up the ugly duckling turned beautiful swan RE4. I even hated RE4 when it was new, but the more I played it the more I understood what the vision for the game was. The people involved with everything post-RE4 never understood what made it work, only downgrading RE's identity further until we hit rock-bottom with 6. This same cynical mining of bedrock continues until the day we hit the molten core of the earth to send a nuke into it, or until we reach biblical hell with the devil himself eager to welcome it's VIP member Capcom in ahead of everyone else.

My grief with RE6 is that it paved the way for the cheap Silent Hills knock-offs RE7 and 8, on top of the absolutely defanged remakes of the classics and braindead shlock which is Requiem. Some will say that they are critically acclaimed games and everyone loves them, but take away the RE title and they become unrecognizable. These games after 6 continue the strategy which killed RE in the first place, which is chasing after market trends and hype while never respecting their own identity. RE4 showed it was possible to be different while still staying relatively Resident Evil, something the games after 4 have utterly failed in. This to me points to soulless corporate checklists having their tyrannical hold over the teams making the games which is nothing new, but it irks me how you, the player, can almost FEEL their cold calculating gaze and hot, greasy breath on your neck like you do with 7 and 8. They are studying you like an insect, poking and prodding every correct neuron in your primitive nervous system that makes you say the correct words of praise they want to hear, but deep down you know there is a thought that won't stop gnawing at you but which you can't say out loud lest your similarly enslaved sibling insects tear you limb-from-limb: "This is not Resident Evil." 

Unfortunately, Capcom struck gold by appealing to the people who were still grieving the death of Silent Hills and scooped up the poor orphans, and so Capcom gracefully dodged having to reflect upon the identity of the RE series as they continue to churn out even more cynical product. I remember truly hating RE6 with all my might when it was new, but seeing the absolute dumps we find ourselves sitting in, I come to realize RE6 comes off as relatively serviceable and even fun, a truly abhorrent statement no one imagined having to utter 10 years hence. This shows the depravity of our desperation, like some addict crawling on the floor, going through every bloody needle and eating stale cigarette stumps to ease the suffering.

I do not say this to be mean, but rather to comment on the absolute creative slump of these past years with regards to gameplay, level design and replayability overall. It feels like there has been a collective lobotomization within game companies where people have no idea what even makes a game fun anymore. The corporate checklist is God, the fiery maws of Baal and Moloch decree that Last of Us is the pinnacle of game design, cutscenes are gameplay, Evil is Good, and war is peace. And we, the hapless slaves, continue to heap ourselves onto this bloody pinnacle with glee. It IS possible to make a good RE game, but Capcom simply lacks the necessary skill and won't admit that Mikami and Kamiya took the series with them in spirit.
 
RE6's mistakes were amateurish but they were honest, like a child learning to walk. We however were sold a marathon. The standards were higher and people knew when gameplay sucked. Now, our standards have been hijacked and they are at rock bottom. We no longer know what makes a game good or enjoyable, and dangling a shiny keychain infront of us as long as it has enough yellow paint on it is what is now deemed acceptable. The unique zany japanese insanity is gone and colonized by the all-devouring westernized "modern audience" as it enslaves the world with it's poisoned opium. The lore is gone, the charm is gone, the characters we once loved turned into grey depressed husks or as cameos in their own series. Capcom is to blame, but so is the naive consumer eager for scraps from their own table for not holding the line and not demanding higher standards. RE6 was the last gasp before we lost the plot.

Resident Evil 6 is a third-person shooter in which players control Chris Redfield, Leon S. Kennedy, Jake Muller, and Ada Wong as they confront the force behind a worldwide bioterrorist attack. I’ve heard about this game being so negative for so long that it basically almost killed the franchise until Resident Evil 7: Biohazard came out. I do try to go into this game with an open mind since I tend to give unpopular opinions on this franchise, but ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, were the fans right because this is absolute bootycheeks. I can at least say that Chris and Jake’s campaigns were some of the better ones in the games because there’s some gameplay variety from Chris’s storyline that I thought was cool to do. As with Jake’s storyline, I was curious to know about Jake Muller since he’s considered to be Wesker’s son, which we never know who the mother is, and his interactions with Sherry Birkin have some interesting moments to spare. The controls are smoother than in the previous game, and The Mercenaries: No Mercy is the best part of the game. It’s just a survival mode where you destroy enough zombies, but you can play as the Left 4 Dead 2 characters, and it was the first time I played online with someone who clearly has so much experience that made it one of the hardest parts of any Resident Evil game, but extremely fun to get through.

I’m sure that the graphics are praised, which character movements wise, it’s smooth, but everything else looks very janky and not very good in comparison to Resident Evil 5. I’m aware of the fact that the stories in Resident Evil are kind of secondary, and so the gameplay is the main seller. So with the gameplay in this, most of it is just so egregiously boring. Leon’s story is probably the worst story out of all of them, with Ada’s story being a close second. Leon’s story does have a lot of zombies, but there were some really stupid moments where I died because of the game, not from my own mistakes. I also did not care at all about Leon’s partner in this, just because she’s a boring character, and her revenge for her sister towards the guy who started it is just standard revenge schlock. There’s also a direct inspiration to Night of the Living Dead that honestly felt more lazy here than actual fun. With Wesker being out of the picture, the game has to find a main antagonist, and what they have for it is such a boring villain that doesn’t do it for me. He’s just a stereotypical villain who has an extreme crush on Ada and basically puts everybody in danger.

With Chris’s storyline, it does seem out of place for Chris to be a complete ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, with a glimpse of him being sexist towards a server for no reason. I mean, granted, yeah, he has trauma from what happened to his crew, but compared to what happened to his partner in Resident Evil 5, I thought he would be more like that instead. It also didn’t feel like a Resident Evil game and felt more like Call of Duty against just random people with weapons as well. Even though Leon is dealing with zombies, it felt more like House of the Dead than Resident Evil, and with this being one of the longest games of the series, it’s extremely bloated and unnecessary, especially with Ada’s story that was only there to fill in some of the story gaps that could’ve been a message to Leon instead. Resident Evil 6 is a really bad game that has some good parts to it, but I was either bored or just annoyed by the creative choices with little to no payoff, especially when characters interact with other characters as well.

3/10
